list.name: Changes
list.comment: Refactor functions: add secure password hashing and validation

task.summary: Default task

API: Add endpoints for match betting and retrieval
This commit introduces the following API endpoints:
- `GetMatchBets`: Retrieves bets for a specific match, separating the user's bet from others. - `GetMatches`: Fetches the 10 most recent, unfinished matches. - `BetMatch`: Allows users to place or update bets on matches.
Additionally, the error status code for "Unknown user" in `InitialBets` and `GetMainTable` has been corrected to 401.

Implemented fetching matches from API
- Added `Match.fromJson` factory to parse match data from JSON. - Replaced placeholder match data with data fetched from `https://obstawiator.pages.dev/API/GetMatches`. - Updated UI to display match ID on tap. - Adjusted background color for better theming. - Removed redundant folder exclusions in `obstawiator.iml`.

Fix typo in GetMatchBets API
The commit corrects a typo in the `GetMatchBets.js` API endpoint where `getMatchBetsResult.results` was incorrectly wrapped in curly braces within the JSON response. This has been rectified to correctly return the results.

Refactor: Update Match model and UI for API consistency
This commit refactors the `Match` model and its corresponding UI representation in `match_list.dart` to align with the backend API.
The following changes were made: - Renamed `matchID` to `ID` - Renamed `team1` to `host` - Renamed `team2` to `guest` - Renamed `score1` to `homeScore` - Renamed `score2` to `awayScore` - Changed `startTime` from `DateTime` to `String` and renamed to `matchStart`
These changes ensure that the frontend model directly maps to the data structure provided by the API, improving consistency and reducing potential mapping errors.

Add home and away scores to GetMatches API endpoint
The GetMatches API endpoint now returns the `homeScore` and `awayScore` fields for finished matches, in addition to the existing fields.

Refactor: Improve match list and introduce match bets page
This commit refactors the match list functionality and introduces a new page for match bets.
**Match List Changes:** - Updated `Match` model: - `ID` is now an `int`. - `matchStart` is now a `DateTime` object. - Modified `_loadMatches` to send `userID` in the request body. - Enhanced UI: - Score display changed from '-' to ':'. - `matchStart` is now formatted as 'dd/MM HH:mm'. - Tapping on a match now navigates to the `MatchBets` page.
**New Match Bets Page (`match_bets.dart`):** - Displays detailed information about a selected match, including host, guest, start time, and scores. - Fetches and displays the current user's bet and other users' bets for the match. - Allows users to place or update their bets via a dialog. - Shows a warning icon if the user hasn't placed a bet and the match is starting soon. - UI elements include: - A header section with match details. - A tappable card to view/modify the user's bet. - A list of other users' bets.
**Other Changes:** - Added `intl` package for date formatting. - Initialized `userID` to 0 in `main.dart`. - Changed the initial route in `main.dart` from `LoginPage` to `MyHomePage` for development/testing purposes.

Fix/API: Corrected parameter binding in BetMatch API
The `placeBet` prepared statement in `functions/API/BetMatch.js` was missing a parameter binding, leading to incorrect data insertion. This commit corrects the `bind` call to include all five required parameters.
Update Deploy branch to latest version

API: GetMainTable now returns user ID
The GetMainTable API endpoint has been updated to include the user ID in its response. This allows for easier identification and tracking of user scores.

Refactor: Enhance main table functionality and user experience
- Implement row tapping for logged-in users to edit their champion and top scorer bets. - Add a dialog for bet editing with input validation and API integration for saving changes. - Clear existing data before fetching new data to prevent duplicates. - Hide checkbox column in the DataTable for a cleaner look. - Highlight the logged-in user's row in the table. - Add an 'ID' field to the `UserStandings` data class. - Improve error handling and user feedback with SnackBars.

Set userID default value to null
The default value of `userID` was changed from `0` to `null` to better represent an uninitialized state.

feat: Add tooltip to user's row in main table
This commit introduces a tooltip to the user's row in the main table. When the user hovers over their own row, a tooltip will now appear with the message "Kliknij, aby edytować swoje typy" (Click to edit your bets). This provides better guidance to the user on how to interact with their own data.
Update deploy version

I18n: Polish translations for match list and match bets
This commit introduces Polish translations for various UI elements within the match list and match bets pages. This includes text for titles, labels, buttons, and messages.
Additionally, the application's initial landing page has been changed from `MyHomePage` to `LoginPage`.
Updated deploy version

Funkcjonalności: - API/GetMatches - dodano rozróżnienie zakończonych i niezakończonych meczów, dodano offset dla zakończonych meczów - API/IsMyBetPlaced - nowy endpoint, który sprawdza czy dany użytkownik obstawił dany mecz

Fix: Improve match list and bet visibility
- Implemented loading of finished matches with a "Load More" button. - Added visual indicators (exclamation marks) for matches starting soon if no bet is placed. - Enhanced the display of other users' bets in a table format, showing points if the match has finished. - Bets of other users are now hidden until the match starts, unless `betVisible` is explicitly set to 1. - Ensured consistent spacing in the match list, even when icons are not present. - Sorted the main table by points in descending order and added a "Place" column. - Corrected the API endpoint for checking if a bet is placed. - General UI improvements and bug fixes related to bet visibility and match loading.

Fix: Allow fetching finished matches with offset 0
Previously, `finishedMatchesOffset: 0` was treated as a falsy value, preventing the retrieval of the first 10 finished matches. This change ensures that an offset of 0 is correctly handled.

Refactor: Improve match list UI and add license info
This commit introduces several improvements to the match list and application UI:
- **Match List:** - Separated finished matches from ongoing/upcoming matches for better organization. - Adjusted match card width based on screen orientation (portrait/landscape) for improved readability. - Ensured match card content is centered. - Optimized API calls for checking if a bet is placed, now only checking for matches on the current day. - Handled cases where `match.ID` might be null in `Match.fromJson`. - Improved feedback messages for loading more finished matches. - **Login Page:** - Adjusted login form width to use full width on devices where height is greater than width (typically mobile portrait mode). - **Main Application:** - Added a "Licencje" (Licenses) option to the settings menu in the AppBar, allowing users to view open-source licenses. - Updated AppBar styling for consistency. - Changed application title to "Obstawiator".

API: Return betVisible for finished matches
This commit updates the `GetMatches` API endpoint to include the `betVisible` field when retrieving finished matches. This allows clients to determine if bets for a finished match should be visible.

Fix: Improved logic for match list and user authentication
This commit introduces several fixes and improvements:
- **Match List:** - Corrected the logic for determining match color based on start time. Matches starting today but in the future will now be correctly color-coded. - Ensured that bet placement checks only occur for matches that haven't started yet. - Addressed an issue where the "Load more finished matches" button would still be visible even if there were fewer than 10 finished matches. It's now hidden in such cases. - Improved the display message when no more finished matches are available to load. - **Login & Registration:** - Standardized email input by converting it to lowercase before sending to the API for both login and registration. This prevents potential authentication issues due to case sensitivity. - Removed unnecessary print statements from the registration process.
Upload to web

Remove unnecessary "Place" column from main table
The "Miejsce" (Place) column, which displayed the rank of each player, has been removed from the main table. This simplifies the table view by removing redundant information, as the player's position is already implicitly shown by their order in the table.
Web updated
Limit the possibility to vote for the best scorer and champion
Added functionality for calculating points after a match ends.
Add a success message to the MatchResult API endpoint.

Refactor: Implement point calculation and UI improvements
This commit introduces a point calculation system for match bets and enhances the user interface in several areas.
- Implemented a `calculatePoints` function in `match_bets.dart` to determine points based on bet accuracy. - Displayed calculated points for the user's bet and other users' bets if the match result is available. - Improved the layout of the user's bet display in `match_bets.dart`, showing "My bet" and "Points earned" as separate cards in a row when applicable. - Made the main table in `main_table.dart` horizontally scrollable and adjusted column spacing for better readability on narrow screens. - Adjusted the width of match cards in `match_list.dart` to utilize more screen space in portrait mode.
Update web

Enhance login form with autofill and email input type
- Wrapped the login form fields in an `AutofillGroup`. - Set `keyboardType` to `TextInputType.emailAddress` for the email field. - Added `autofillHints` for both email and password fields. - Called `TextInput.finishAutofillContext()` on password field's `onEditingComplete`. - Updated `intl` package to version `0.20.2`. - Updated project description in `pubspec.yaml`.

Allow updating initial bet with point deduction if not too late
- If the current time is before a specific deadline (1752692400000) and the user exists: - Check if the user's initial bet has been changed before. - If not changed or `betChanged` is null, update the bet, deduct 5 points, and set `betChanged` to 1. - If already changed, update the bet without point deduction. - If the current time is past the initial deadline (1751472000000) but before the extended deadline, and the user doesn't meet the above conditions for updating, the existing "too late" error is returned. - If the current time is past the extended deadline, the "too late" error is returned.

Refactor: Enhance bet submission with winner selection
This commit introduces the ability for users to select a winner when placing a bet.
- The `submitBet` function in `match_bets.dart` now accepts an optional `winner` parameter. - The request body for the `BetMatch` API endpoint now includes the `winner` field. - The "Place Bet" dialog has been updated to include checkboxes for selecting either the host or guest as the winner. - The selected winner is stored in the `_selectedWinner` state variable and passed to the `submitBet` function. - Debug logging has been added to print the request body before submitting a bet.

Add support for betting on a winner in BetMatch API
This commit updates the BetMatch API to allow users to specify a winner when placing or updating a bet.
- The `UPDATE` and `INSERT` queries in `BetMatch.js` now conditionally include the `winner` field if it's provided in the request body.

Add "winner" field to GetMatchBets API response
This commit modifies the GetMatchBets API endpoint to include the "winner" field in its response.
- The SQL queries used to fetch match bets and user bets have been updated to select the `winner` column from the `BetMatch` table. - The response now includes the "winner" field for both the user's bet and other users' bets.

Include `isGroupStage` in GetMatches API response
The `isGroupStage` field is now included in the data returned by the `GetMatches` API endpoint for both finished and upcoming matches.

Refactor: Adapt point calculation and UI for non-group stage matches
This commit introduces distinct point calculation logic and UI adjustments for group stage versus non-group stage matches.
**Point Calculation (match_bets.dart):** - `calculatePoints` now accepts optional `betWinner` and `actualWinner` parameters. - For non-group stage matches: - Correct winner prediction: +2 points. - Exact score prediction: +4 points. - These points are additive (e.g., correct winner and exact score = 6 points). - Group stage point calculation remains unchanged.
**UI & Logic (match_bets.dart):** - Added `isGroupStage` and `winner` parameters to the `MatchBets` widget. - When placing a bet for a non-group stage match: - A checkbox for selecting the winner (host/guest) is displayed in the betting dialog. - Winner selection is now mandatory; a warning message is shown if not selected. - A disclaimer "Zakład na wynik dotyczy tylko regulaminowego czasu gry." (Bet on the score applies only to regular playing time) is displayed. - The user's bet display now includes the predicted winner for non-group stage matches. - The table of other users' bets now includes a "Zwycięzca" (Winner) column for non-group stage matches. - Column widths in the bets table are adjusted based on whether it's a group stage match.
**Match List (match_list.dart):** - `Match` model now includes `isGroupStage` (boolean) and `winner` (boolean, nullable for host/guest) properties. - `Match.fromJson` updated to parse these new fields. - `isGroupStage` and `winner` are passed to the `MatchBets` page. - Match start time display format changed to include the day of the week (e.g., "Poniedziałek, 01/07 21:00") using Polish locale. - Initialized Polish locale for date formatting.

Check if user exists before checking for bet
This commit modifies the `IsMyBetPlaced` API endpoint to first verify the existence of the user before checking if they have placed a bet on the specified match.
If the user exists and has placed a bet, the API now returns the bet details including the `winner`.

Refactor: Adjust winner type and bet checking logic
This commit changes the `winner` property type from `bool?` to `int?` in `MatchBets` and `Match` classes to allow for more than two outcomes (e.g., draw).
- In `match_bets.dart`, updated `calculatePoints` function to reflect the change in `winner` type. - In `match_list.dart`: - Updated the `Match` class constructor and properties for the new `winner` type. - Modified the logic in `fetchMatchBets` to check for `userBetHome` and `userBetAway` being non-null to determine `hasBet`, or if a `winner` is already present in the response. - Adjusted the condition for displaying the warning icon to only show if `hasBet` is false and the match is in the future.

Refactor: Adjust winner type and bet checking logic
This commit changes the `winner` property type from `bool?` to `int?` in `MatchBets` and `Match` classes to allow for more than two outcomes (e.g., draw).
- In `match_bets.dart`, updated `calculatePoints` function to reflect the change in `winner` type. - In `match_list.dart`: - Updated the `Match` class constructor and properties for the new `winner` type. - Modified the logic in `fetchMatchBets` to check for `userBetHome` and `userBetAway` being non-null to determine `hasBet`, or if a `winner` is already present in the response. - Adjusted the condition for displaying the warning icon to only show if `hasBet` is false and the match is in the future.

Refactor: Simplify hasBet logic in match_list.dart
The `hasBet` property for a match is now determined by checking if `data['userBet']` is not null when `data` is a map. The previous check for `data['winner']` has been removed.
Additionally, a print statement for debugging the response body has been removed.

Return only matchID from IsMyBetPlaced API endpoint
The `winner` field is not used in the IsMyBetPlaced API endpoint, so it has been removed from the SQL query to optimize performance.

Refactor: Update autofill hints for login form
- Changed `AutofillHints.email` to `[AutofillHints.newUsername, AutofillHints.username]` for the email field. - Changed `AutofillHints.password` to `[AutofillHints.newPassword, AutofillHints.password]` for the password field.

Feat: Implement MatchResultKO API endpoint for knockout matches
This commit introduces a new API endpoint, `MatchResultKO.js`, designed to handle the results of knockout stage matches.
The endpoint performs the following actions: - Updates the match score, winner, and sets `matchFinished` to 1 in the `Matches` table. - Iterates through all users. - For each user who has placed a bet on the match: - Calculates points based on the bet: - 2 points for correctly guessing the winner. - 4 points for correctly guessing the exact score (home and away). - Updates the user's total points in the `UserScores` table. - Returns a JSON response with a success message and a 201 status code. - Redirects to a placeholder URL if the request body is invalid.

Refactor: Improve point calculation for non-group stage matches
This commit updates the point calculation logic in `match_bets.dart` for matches that are not in the group stage.
- Modified `calculatePoints` function to directly use integer values for `betWinner` and `actualWinner` (0 for home, 1 for away) instead of boolean. - Updated calls to `calculatePoints` to pass the integer winner values. - Adjusted the column width for the "winner" column in the bets table to `FlexColumnWidth` for non-group stage matches..

Refactor: Improve point calculation for non-group stage matches
This commit updates the point calculation logic in `match_bets.dart` for matches that are not in the group stage.
- Modified `calculatePoints` function to directly use integer values for `betWinner` and `actualWinner` (0 for home, 1 for away) instead of boolean. - Updated calls to `calculatePoints` to pass the integer winner values. - Adjusted the column width for the "winner" column in the bets table to `FlexColumnWidth` for non-group stage matches..

Add API endpoint to update final results and points
This commit introduces a new API endpoint, `/API/FinalResults`, to handle the submission of final tournament results (champion and top scorer).
The endpoint performs the following actions: - Updates the `ChampionBet` table with the provided champion and top scorer. - Iterates through all users in the `Users` table. - For each user, retrieves their champion and top scorer bets from the `UserScores` table. - Calculates points based on the correctness of their bets (10 points for correct champion, 5 points for correct top scorer). - Updates the `points` field in the `UserScores` table for each user. - Returns a JSON response with a success message and a 201 status code. - If the request body is not valid JSON, it redirects to a placeholder YouTube video.

Implement Cloudflare Workers backend and refactor Flutter UI components
This commit introduces a comprehensive backend implementation using Cloudflare Workers and refactors the Flutter application to use standardized UI components.
Backend Changes: - Added `functions/worker.js` as a unified entry point for API logic, including password hashing (PBKDF2) and verification. - Implemented multiple API endpoints in `functions/API/`: - `Register.js` & `Login.js`: User authentication and registration. - `GetMatches.js` & `GetMatchBets.js`: Retrieval of match data and user bets. - `BetMatch.js` & `InitialBets.js`: Submission of match-specific and tournament-wide bets. - `MatchResult.js`, `MatchResultKO.js`, & `FinalResults.js`: Logic for updating scores and calculating user points based on match outcomes. - `IsMyBetPlaced.js`: Helper to check existing bets. - Updated `GetMainTable.js` to include user IDs in results and standardized error response codes (401 instead of 404).
Frontend Changes: - Refactored `lib/main.dart` to replace functional UI builders with `ObstawiatorAppBar` and `ObstawiatorBottomNavigationBar` StatelessWidget classes. - Updated multiple pages (`match_bets.dart`, `match_list.dart`, `main_table.dart`, etc.) to use the new constant constructor-based app bar and navigation bar components. - Improved logout logic to clear `userID` and reset the navigation stack to the login page. - Standardized the use of `ThemeData` across the application.
Refactor functions: add secure password hashing and validation

MESSAGE.value: Web updated
Limit the possibility to vote for the best scorer and champion
Added functionality for calculating points after a match ends.
Add a success message to the MatchResult API endpoint.

Refactor: Implement point calculation and UI improvements
This commit introduces a point calculation system for match bets and enhances the user interface in several areas.
- Implemented a `calculatePoints` function in `match_bets.dart` to determine points based on bet accuracy. - Displayed calculated points for the user's bet and other users' bets if the match result is available. - Improved the layout of the user's bet display in `match_bets.dart`, showing "My bet" and "Points earned" as separate cards in a row when applicable. - Made the main table in `main_table.dart` horizontally scrollable and adjusted column spacing for better readability on narrow screens. - Adjusted the width of match cards in `match_list.dart` to utilize more screen space in portrait mode.
Update web

Enhance login form with autofill and email input type
- Wrapped the login form fields in an `AutofillGroup`. - Set `keyboardType` to `TextInputType.emailAddress` for the email field. - Added `autofillHints` for both email and password fields. - Called `TextInput.finishAutofillContext()` on password field's `onEditingComplete`. - Updated `intl` package to version `0.20.2`. - Updated project description in `pubspec.yaml`.

Allow updating initial bet with point deduction if not too late
- If the current time is before a specific deadline (1752692400000) and the user exists: - Check if the user's initial bet has been changed before. - If not changed or `betChanged` is null, update the bet, deduct 5 points, and set `betChanged` to 1. - If already changed, update the bet without point deduction. - If the current time is past the initial deadline (1751472000000) but before the extended deadline, and the user doesn't meet the above conditions for updating, the existing "too late" error is returned. - If the current time is past the extended deadline, the "too late" error is returned.

Refactor: Enhance bet submission with winner selection
This commit introduces the ability for users to select a winner when placing a bet.
- The `submitBet` function in `match_bets.dart` now accepts an optional `winner` parameter. - The request body for the `BetMatch` API endpoint now includes the `winner` field. - The "Place Bet" dialog has been updated to include checkboxes for selecting either the host or guest as the winner. - The selected winner is stored in the `_selectedWinner` state variable and passed to the `submitBet` function. - Debug logging has been added to print the request body before submitting a bet.

Add support for betting on a winner in BetMatch API
This commit updates the BetMatch API to allow users to specify a winner when placing or updating a bet.
- The `UPDATE` and `INSERT` queries in `BetMatch.js` now conditionally include the `winner` field if it's provided in the request body.

Add "winner" field to GetMatchBets API response
This commit modifies the GetMatchBets API endpoint to include the "winner" field in its response.
- The SQL queries used to fetch match bets and user bets have been updated to select the `winner` column from the `BetMatch` table. - The response now includes the "winner" field for both the user's bet and other users' bets.

Include `isGroupStage` in GetMatches API response
The `isGroupStage` field is now included in the data returned by the `GetMatches` API endpoint for both finished and upcoming matches.

Refactor: Adapt point calculation and UI for non-group stage matches
This commit introduces distinct point calculation logic and UI adjustments for group stage versus non-group stage matches.
**Point Calculation (match_bets.dart):** - `calculatePoints` now accepts optional `betWinner` and `actualWinner` parameters. - For non-group stage matches: - Correct winner prediction: +2 points. - Exact score prediction: +4 points. - These points are additive (e.g., correct winner and exact score = 6 points). - Group stage point calculation remains unchanged.
**UI & Logic (match_bets.dart):** - Added `isGroupStage` and `winner` parameters to the `MatchBets` widget. - When placing a bet for a non-group stage match: - A checkbox for selecting the winner (host/guest) is displayed in the betting dialog. - Winner selection is now mandatory; a warning message is shown if not selected. - A disclaimer "Zakład na wynik dotyczy tylko regulaminowego czasu gry." (Bet on the score applies only to regular playing time) is displayed. - The user's bet display now includes the predicted winner for non-group stage matches. - The table of other users' bets now includes a "Zwycięzca" (Winner) column for non-group stage matches. - Column widths in the bets table are adjusted based on whether it's a group stage match.
**Match List (match_list.dart):** - `Match` model now includes `isGroupStage` (boolean) and `winner` (boolean, nullable for host/guest) properties. - `Match.fromJson` updated to parse these new fields. - `isGroupStage` and `winner` are passed to the `MatchBets` page. - Match start time display format changed to include the day of the week (e.g., "Poniedziałek, 01/07 21:00") using Polish locale. - Initialized Polish locale for date formatting.

Check if user exists before checking for bet
This commit modifies the `IsMyBetPlaced` API endpoint to first verify the existence of the user before checking if they have placed a bet on the specified match.
If the user exists and has placed a bet, the API now returns the bet details including the `winner`.

Refactor: Adjust winner type and bet checking logic
This commit changes the `winner` property type from `bool?` to `int?` in `MatchBets` and `Match` classes to allow for more than two outcomes (e.g., draw).
- In `match_bets.dart`, updated `calculatePoints` function to reflect the change in `winner` type. - In `match_list.dart`: - Updated the `Match` class constructor and properties for the new `winner` type. - Modified the logic in `fetchMatchBets` to check for `userBetHome` and `userBetAway` being non-null to determine `hasBet`, or if a `winner` is already present in the response. - Adjusted the condition for displaying the warning icon to only show if `hasBet` is false and the match is in the future.

Refactor: Simplify hasBet logic in match_list.dart
The `hasBet` property for a match is now determined by checking if `data['userBet']` is not null when `data` is a map. The previous check for `data['winner']` has been removed.
Additionally, a print statement for debugging the response body has been removed.

Return only matchID from IsMyBetPlaced API endpoint
The `winner` field is not used in the IsMyBetPlaced API endpoint, so it has been removed from the SQL query to optimize performance.

Refactor: Update autofill hints for login form
- Changed `AutofillHints.email` to `[AutofillHints.newUsername, AutofillHints.username]` for the email field. - Changed `AutofillHints.password` to `[AutofillHints.newPassword, AutofillHints.password]` for the password field.

Feat: Implement MatchResultKO API endpoint for knockout matches
This commit introduces a new API endpoint, `MatchResultKO.js`, designed to handle the results of knockout stage matches.
The endpoint performs the following actions: - Updates the match score, winner, and sets `matchFinished` to 1 in the `Matches` table. - Iterates through all users. - For each user who has placed a bet on the match: - Calculates points based on the bet: - 2 points for correctly guessing the winner. - 4 points for correctly guessing the exact score (home and away). - Updates the user's total points in the `UserScores` table. - Returns a JSON response with a success message and a 201 status code. - Redirects to a placeholder URL if the request body is invalid.

Refactor: Improve point calculation for non-group stage matches
This commit updates the point calculation logic in `match_bets.dart` for matches that are not in the group stage.
- Modified `calculatePoints` function to directly use integer values for `betWinner` and `actualWinner` (0 for home, 1 for away) instead of boolean. - Updated calls to `calculatePoints` to pass the integer winner values. - Adjusted the column width for the "winner" column in the bets table to `FlexColumnWidth` for non-group stage matches..

Add API endpoint to update final results and points
This commit introduces a new API endpoint, `/API/FinalResults`, to handle the submission of final tournament results (champion and top scorer).
The endpoint performs the following actions: - Updates the `ChampionBet` table with the provided champion and top scorer. - Iterates through all users in the `Users` table. - For each user, retrieves their champion and top scorer bets from the `UserScores` table. - Calculates points based on the correctness of their bets (10 points for correct champion, 5 points for correct top scorer). - Updates the `points` field in the `UserScores` table for each user. - Returns a JSON response with a success message and a 201 status code. - If the request body is not valid JSON, it redirects to a placeholder YouTube video.

Implement Cloudflare Workers backend and refactor Flutter UI components
This commit introduces a comprehensive backend implementation using Cloudflare Workers and refactors the Flutter application to use standardized UI components.
Backend Changes: - Added `functions/worker.js` as a unified entry point for API logic, including password hashing (PBKDF2) and verification. - Implemented multiple API endpoints in `functions/API/`: - `Register.js` & `Login.js`: User authentication and registration. - `GetMatches.js` & `GetMatchBets.js`: Retrieval of match data and user bets. - `BetMatch.js` & `InitialBets.js`: Submission of match-specific and tournament-wide bets. - `MatchResult.js`, `MatchResultKO.js`, & `FinalResults.js`: Logic for updating scores and calculating user points based on match outcomes. - `IsMyBetPlaced.js`: Helper to check existing bets. - Updated `GetMainTable.js` to include user IDs in results and standardized error response codes (401 instead of 404).
Frontend Changes: - Refactored `lib/main.dart` to replace functional UI builders with `ObstawiatorAppBar` and `ObstawiatorBottomNavigationBar` StatelessWidget classes. - Updated multiple pages (`match_bets.dart`, `match_list.dart`, `main_table.dart`, etc.) to use the new constant constructor-based app bar and navigation bar components. - Improved logout logic to clear `userID` and reset the navigation stack to the login page. - Standardized the use of `ThemeData` across the application.
Refactor functions: add secure password hashing and validation

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: Refactor functions: add secure password hashing and validation
